title: Level 19.2
wikitext:

{{Audio|file=Sourire d'Avril - Maurice Depret.mp3}} {{Background|file=Level 254..webp}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 19.2: "Torturous Halls"'''}} {{Class Custom Image|class=Deadzone|hexagon=🕱|header=Class: |safety=Dangerously Unsafe|security=Dangerously Unstable|entity=Numerous Environmental Hazards|bg-image=Level 254..webp|bg-size=1300|text-color=green}} == '''Description''' == {{Blurimage|file=Level 254..webp|caption=A typical hallway in Level 19.2.|hovertext=Wander upon derelict halls.}} {{Blurimage|file=Level 254's new hallway.webp|caption=A recently discovered hallway in Level 19.2.|hovertext=Give yourself up.}} Level 19.2 is a sublevel of [[Level 19]]. 「 Level 19.2 is a sprawling complex of endless, somewhat derelict hotel halls plagued by faulty lighting. Wanderers always enter through a roughly average sized hotel room, though the specific wallpaper differs for each individual. Lighting throughout the level flickers unpredictably, sometimes sparking. Unlike most levels, lights are never mounted on the ceiling. Doors vary in material, ranging from wood to steel. Steel doors are uniquely dangerous because they have a tendency to fall over, crushing wanderers beneath their weight. Exit signs can be found occasionally throughout the level, usually turned off or flickering. 」 「 Remaining in one place in Level 19.2 may cause severe and irreversible sanity drain, so it is advised not to stay long. The environment is unsafe and unstable; heavy ceiling tiles collapse to reveal exposed wiring, walls may cave in without warning, and floors can give way beneath wanderers. These hazards make the level extremely dangerous and generally avoided. 」 「 Conditions within the level are notably hostile. The temperature fluctuates wildly from around 20°C68°F to -40°CNo translation is needed, as -40° is the same between Fahrenheit and Celsius. . This, alongside the unstable architecture, causes major environmental risk. To add on to this risk, supplies are scarce, with only the occasional [[Object 1|Almond Water]] or doggie bag of snacks. The wallpaper is highly temperature-sensitive; sections without wallpaper are usually frigid, so give those areas a wide berth. Barricaded doors occasionally may hold the exit, but their metal is filled with raw electricity, so it is unwise to try. It is believed these doors are the main power system. 」 「 The hotel rooms other than the one wanderers spawn in are usually fully furnished, but with a distinct absence of supplies. Any attempt to break or damage a painting results in the painting spontaneously combusting, usually killing the wanderer. After a few moments, the painting will regenerate in a strange, unnatural way, restoring itself as if nothing happened. Heaters appear rarely but are extremely unstable, sometimes exploding due to the excessive flow of electricity from nearby wires. Hotel rooms beyond the initial spawn point are far more dangerous, frequently containing coolers, puddles of [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]], and temperature-based anomaliesMost notably, in one room the temperature was recorded to somehow be lower than Absolute Zero.. Survival within this environment requires extreme caution and constant movement, as nearly every feature carries hidden threats. 」 == Entities == 「 Due to the hazardous level conditions, no entities can exist here. 」 == Phenomena == {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=1|effectiveness=5|danger=0}} The Level 19.2 Effect, also known as Forgetfulness, is a phenomenon where people under its influence wholeheartedly believe that the level is vastly different to its true form. People affected by the Level 19.2 Effect cannot be swayed and will be steadfast in their beliefs, regardless of its detriment to personal bonds or leadership position. By estimates, roughly 2% of all Backrooms denizens are affected, including high-ranking officials in multiple groups. Surprisingly, beliefs of the people affected are usually concrete, and affected persons believe: * That Level 19.2 is safe and habitable, which it is not; * That Level 19.2 has a fresh and clean appearance, in contrast to its dilapidated state; * That Level 19.2 has several sustained communities, which it does not; * And that Level 19.2 is practically a paradise. Formerly believed to be misremembering the former state of [[Level 350]], this has been disproven, as newcomers who had never learned of Level 19.2's existence nor 350's still adamantly believed these erroneous facts. These individuals should be disregarded on matters pertaining to the level. == Colonies/Outposts == === Nomads === *A group of 12-13 nomads *Accepting of new members *Wander Level 19.2 to find supplies and the occasional resting spot *Open to trading == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === Entering abnormally cold hotel rooms in any hotel-based level (ex. [[Level 5]], [[Level 350]]) and falling asleep will lead one to wake up here. Entering a vent in '''"The Lobby Area"''' of [[Level 960]] also leads one here. Certain factors may cause levels with primary hallway to segue into this level. Notably, in [[Level 634]], a door affixed with 'HOTEL' scrawled on in faded grey sharpie may appear; opening it will knock one out and cause them to wake up in the level. === Exits === There are a plethora of ''recorded'' exit methods, but virtually none of them have been repeated more than thrice. The best say is simply to test your environment for possible exits. {{Collapse|initial=NEW NOTIFICATION!|final=No new notifications.|body= This file has been updated. View new file?
